thesis: Best-in-class cloud observability platform with 32% revenue growth, 80% gross margins, and 29% FCF margins. However, at 20x+ EV/Revenue the valuation already reflects exceptional quality. Analyst consensus targets (~$220) imply slight downside. Risk/reward is balanced, not asymmetric. Wait for pullback to $200-$210 or breakout above $240.

drivers: AI infrastructure monitoring TAM expands by $5B+
Datadog captures 20%+ share of AI/LLM observability
NRR stays >120%, revenue growth reaccelerates to 30%
Multiple expands to 25x EV/Revenue on AI enthusiasm

risks: Cloud optimization headwinds return, growth drops to 15%
Dynatrace or open-source alternatives gain pricing power
Multiple compresses to 12x EV/Revenue on deceleration
SBC dilution accelerates, GAAP margins disappoint

base_case.assumptions: Steady 20–25% growth with 22–25% operating margins. Platform consolidation continues but no acceleration. Multiple compresses modestly to 15–18x as company matures. Stock tracks fundamentals with limited alpha.

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 Earnings — guidance update and AI traction metrics
FY2027 Guidance — first look at next-year growth trajectory

key_risks: Valuation risk: 20x+ EV/Revenue priced for perfection
Growth deceleration: cloud optimization or macro weakness
Competition: Dynatrace, Grafana, bundled cloud monitoring
SBC dilution: GAAP margins significantly lower than non-GAAP
